mission.title: Install and Configure Tremor on Kubernetes
mission.description: Tremor is an event- or stream-processing system designed for high-volumetric data, providing low latency and efficient resource usage. Installing Tremor allows you to handle ETL workloads effectively, making it a valuable tool for data processing in production environments.

steps.title: Create a Namespace
steps.description: Create a dedicated namespace for Tremor to run in. This helps to isolate the resources used by Tremor from other applications in your cluster.

steps.title: Apply the Tremor Deployment
steps.description: Deploy Tremor using the provided Kubernetes manifests. Ensure you are using a specific version instead of the latest.

steps.title: Apply the Tremor Service
steps.description: Expose the Tremor deployment via a service to allow communication with other components.

steps.title: Verify the Installation
steps.description: Check that the Tremor pods are running correctly in the tremor namespace.

uninstall.title: Remove Tremor Resources
uninstall.description: To uninstall Tremor, delete the deployment and service using kubectl.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Tremor
upgrade.description: To upgrade Tremor, apply the new version of the deployment and service manifests.

troubleshooting.title: Pod Not Starting
troubleshooting.description:
If the Tremor pod is not starting, check the pod logs for errors using:
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n tremor
```

troubleshooting.title: Service Not Accessible
troubleshooting.description:
If you cannot access the Tremor service, ensure that the service type is set correctly (e.g., ClusterIP or NodePort) and check the service configuration with:
```bash
kubectl describe service tremor -n tremor
```

troubleshooting.title: Insufficient Resources
troubleshooting.description: If the Tremor pods are in a pending state, it may be due to insufficient resources. Check the resource requests and limits in the deployment manifest and adjust them accordingly.

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Tremor will have all pods running in the 'tremor' namespace, and you will be able to access the service via the forwarded port. Resource limits will be configured, ensuring optimal performance.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have kubectl installed and configured to communicate with your Kubernetes cluster.
